lesson: The parity-loop and improve gates referenced pre-commit, which is not installed in pipy and which has no hooks config; pipy uses prek and just check is the real always-on gate. Reference prek (not pre-commit) and run it only when a .pre-commit-config.yaml is present.

The improve-body mark example placed the global option --repo after the mark subcommand, which argparse rejects: global --repo/--ledger must precede the subcommand. Show parity_lessons.py mark <id> applied --sha <SHA> (default --repo is the CWD) and note global options come before the subcommand.

When adding or renaming an extension UI API, scan every primitive enumeration in docs/specs/backlog/parity tables for duplicate or stale entries before final review; repeated one-line lists are easy to drift even when implementation and tests are correct.
The parity runner should surface post-loop child caveats, such as an incomplete full gate, in run.jsonl instead of only preserving them in improve-postloop.log.
After unattended runs, auditability of the no-push guarantee needs explicit pre/post remote-tracking ref evidence because origin/main can update outside the child log narrative.

When a review finding names a repeated helper call, verify the final diff patches the intended occurrence before re-review; a broad search/replace can accidentally guard a nearby call while leaving the reviewed path unchanged.

gap: extension ctx.ui theme controls (rich-UI item E)
lesson: Adding a member to a @runtime_checkable Protocol (e.g. ExtensionUiDriver) fails just check typecheck for every FULLY-TYPED test fake (they must implement the new method), while UNTYPED fakes are exempt from mypy structural-completeness checking; a new fake that defines only the added method still fails. When growing such a protocol, update each complete typed fake AND give partial new fakes a complete untyped no-op base class.

gap: extension ctx.ui theme controls (rich-UI item E)
lesson: Extension ctx.ui theme reads (theme/get_all_themes/get_theme) and set_theme resolve the real persisted NativeThemeStore via resolve_active_theme_name, so default-theme assertions are flaky against developer/CI chrome state. Clearing PIPY_THEME alone is insufficient; an autouse fixture must also set PIPY_NATIVE_THEME_PATH to a fresh nonexistent tmp file so resolution deterministically falls through to the built-in default and nothing persists.

When adding new user docs pages, update the Zensical nav in zensical.toml before treating docs-build as clean; otherwise cross-links to the new pages can be reported as missing site pages even though the Markdown files exist.
CHANGELOG.md Unreleased entries are rendered oldest-first; for a newer slice, append its release-note entry after older entries in the relevant section instead of prepending it.

When adding provider-visible tool filters, preserve the unfiltered sentinel separately from an explicit active-name set so extension reload can add new tools without being hidden by a stale snapshot; final reviews should check reload paths after startup filtering changes.

When asking Claude for a noninteractive plan or diff review, include the relevant plan/diff content directly or allow tools; a tools-disabled review without file content can fabricate repo/path assumptions or refuse the verdict, wasting review rounds.

For documentation-only parity slices, verify optional slash-command arguments against the actual command dispatcher, not only help/spec tables; a bracketed argument in docs can be stale even when the bare command ships.

For extension API metadata parity slices, pin the Pi reference field list, optionality, and derived identifiers in the plan before implementation; otherwise reviewers must catch whether the slice is preserving or silently dropping future auth data.

For extension delivery queues, plan and test lifecycle boundaries explicitly: next-turn context must be removed from long-lived history after the target turn, cleared on session switches, and triggerTurn must be idle-only with strict boolean option handling.

For provider request-shape parity slices, pin each thinking/auth field's forced DEFAULT VALUE in the plan, not just its presence/optionality. A "Pi forces X" default in the source is usually load-bearing: e.g. Anthropic display:"summarized" matters because the adaptive models' API default is "omitted", so dropping it as a simplification is a behavioral divergence (no thinking summary) the different-family reviewer will flag. Extend the existing field-list/optionality pinning rule to cover default values and whether they diverge from the upstream API default.

When a new behavioral split is keyed on model-id markers (e.g. adaptive vs budget thinking for opus-4-6/4-7/4-8, sonnet-4-6), check whether shared test fixtures default to a now-special model id. The anthropic construction fixture defaulted to claude-opus-4-7, so adding adaptive behavior silently flipped two budget-path tests to the adaptive shape and broke them. Re-point baseline/budget-path tests to a non-special id (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-5) to preserve that coverage, and add separate tests for the new path, rather than letting existing tests change which path they exercise.

For a request-shape slice that changes ONE field of a multi-field provider request path, the different-family reviewer will check whether the OTHER Pi fields on that same path are matched. A plan that calls its pinned field list COMPLETE invites an ISSUES verdict naming an unrelated sibling field (e.g. bedrock thinking display vs Pi non-adaptive anthropic_beta interleaved-thinking). Label the pinned list as the fields THIS slice changes, and explicitly scope out the other Pi fields on the path, stating whether each is already matched or a pre-existing separate gap. Also cite Pi comment scope exactly when justifying a forced-default-vs-API-default divergence rather than generalizing across a model family.

gap: anthropic-messages explicit thinking:{type:disabled} for reasoning model with thinking off
lesson: A parity gap whose source groups multiple adapters of one body family (the gap audit said "neither anthropic-messages nor amazon-bedrock emits thinking:{type:disabled}") can require ASYMMETRIC handling, because Pi behavior diverges per adapter: Pi anthropic.ts emits an explicit thinking:{type:disabled} for a reasoning-capable model with thinking off (streamSimpleAnthropic thinkingEnabled:false -> buildParams under if(model.reasoning)), but Pi amazon-bedrock.ts buildAdditionalModelRequestFields returns undefined (OMITS thinking fields, no disabled shape) for the same off/non-reasoning case. In Phase 1/2 verify each named adapter against Pi source independently and pin per-adapter behavior; do not trust the audit collective framing, which can overstate scope. Correct the gap-source doc when one side is already Pi-correct (pipy bedrock already omitted) so the slice ships only the adapter that truly diverged.

Catalog-construction tests pass a fake env dict to _resolve/resolve_construction for AUTH resolution, but adapter fields backed by an os.environ default_factory (e.g. azure api_version) do NOT read that dict - they read the real process env. So a test env entry like AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION that happens to equal the old hardcoded default is a silent no-op, masquerading as an honored override. When changing such an adapter default, check whether construction tests env dicts actually reach the field; assert the new default explicitly and drop the misleading env entry (or monkeypatch os.environ if you truly want to test the override path).

For a provider auth/request-shape slice where Pi DELEGATES the concrete HTTP shape to a third-party SDK (e.g. google-vertex.ts calls @google/genai GoogleGenAI), the Pi source file does NOT reveal the load-bearing wire fields: the Vertex Express api-key path only shows createClientWithApiKey({vertexai,apiKey,apiVersion}), not the host, URL path, or auth header. Those must be pinned from the VENDORED SDK under pi-mono/node_modules (here @google/genai/dist/node/index.cjs: baseUrl=https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/ for the apiKey/global case ~12978-12982; shouldPrependVertexProjectPath returns false when apiKey is set ~13104 so no projects/{p}/locations/{l} segment; NodeAuth.addKeyHeader sends x-goog-api-key not Authorization ~19480). Pinning only from Pi's own source leaves the express host (global vs regional), the header name, and the dropped project/location path unspecified -> wrong endpoint risk. Extend the field-pinning rule: when Pi hands the request to an SDK, read the SDK build in node_modules to pin host/path/header, and cite those exact lines in the plan.

For a provider request-shape slice, first locate WHERE Pi computes the shape. Some families key off catalog/model-registry fields (anthropic thinkingLevelMap -> reasoning_effort); others compute it INSIDE the provider from model-id family regexes with no catalog field at all (google.ts getThinkingLevel/getGoogleBudget/getDisabledThinkingConfig branch on /gemini-3.*-pro/, /gemma-?4/, id.includes(2.5-pro) etc, and Pi google models carry NO thinkingLevelMap). For the in-provider-regex kind: (1) put the per-family logic in the pipy adapter, not construction; (2) pin that the catalog spec carries no thinkingLevelMap so map_thinking_level returns the level verbatim == Pi clamped effort, and note that adding one would break budget/level keying; (3) reuse existing resolved intent fields (reasoning_effort + thinking_disabled already added by the anthropic disabled-thinking slice) instead of adding new ResolvedConstruction fields; (4) pin that Pi sentinel values like getGoogleBudget -1 (dynamic budget) are legitimate emitted values, not error sentinels. Scope sibling families (google-vertex uses a THINKING_LEVEL_MAP variant) as separate follow-ons.

The pi-review-loop bundle is DIFF-ONLY (## Staged diff hunks, no full-file content), so the reviewer cannot see unchanged lines outside the hunks. The classic false positive: you add new module-level re.compile(...) constants, but import re is unchanged at the top of the file and thus invisible -> a [Critical] verdict claiming "the diff does not add import re; importing will NameError (if not already imported)". just check is green and the module imports fine, but you still cannot self-grade past an ISSUES verdict. Durable fix that makes the diff self-evidently correct: co-locate the new module-level construct with an EXISTING same-kind one (place new compiled-regex constants immediately after the file_s existing _PLACEHOLDER_API_KEY_RE = re.compile(...) constant) so a context line in the same hunk shows re is already used in unchanged code, refuting the concern. This pattern (new constant adjacent to an existing constant of the same module dependency) pre-empts the whole class of diff-blindness false positives and cost one extra review round here. Re-running the review without changing the code repeated the identical finding (it is not just stochastic), so re-run is not a reliable escape; restructure for diff-locality instead.

When porting a Pi `model.thinkingLevelMap?.off ?? "none"` after a `!== null` gate (or any Pi `obj?.key ?? default`), an ABSENT key and a key mapped to null are DISTINCT outcomes: absent -> emit the default, explicit null -> suppress. Python `dict.get("key", default)` conflates absent and value-None, so it cannot reproduce this; test membership first (`if "key" not in m: return default; return m["key"]`). Do NOT reach for an `object()` sentinel as the `.get` default to disambiguate: mypy cannot narrow `object` back to the map value type and `just check` typecheck fails with `Incompatible return value type (got object, expected str | None)`. The membership form types cleanly and matches Pi exactly. Also: the thinking OFF/UNSET state is itself a request-shape field to match (Pi emits an explicit disable like openrouter reasoning:{effort:"none"} / anthropic thinking:{type:disabled}), and its off-branch must be gated on the raw off/unset level (not merely map_thinking_level returning None, which also fires for non-reasoning models and unsupported clamped levels).

When a provider request-shape field is gated by a SECONDARY compat flag (deepseek thinkingFormat emits thinking:{type} always, but only adds top-level reasoning_effort when compat.supportsReasoningEffort), pin that secondary flag's resolution path SEPARATELY from the format flag. Pi's getCompat resolves every compat field INDEPENDENTLY (thinkingFormat ?? detected, supportsReasoningEffort ?? detected — different detectCompat predicates), so the two are not coupled: an explicit compat.thinkingFormat="deepseek" on a provider/baseUrl that Pi's detectCompat EXCLUDES from supportsReasoningEffort (isGrok/isZai/isMoonshot/isTogether/isCloudflareAiGateway/isNvidia/isAntLing) yields thinkingFormat=deepseek AND supportsReasoningEffort=false → Pi emits thinking:{type:enabled} with NO reasoning_effort. A helper that defaults the secondary flag to True "because deepseek detection implies it" is wrong and the different-family reviewer flags it (cost one plan-review round here). Implement the secondary flag as its own faithful predicate (explicit bool wins, else the exclusion list) — a single bounded predicate, not a full detectCompat port — and add a test for the explicit-format-on-excluded-provider mismatch. Extend the field-list/optionality/default pinning rule: also pin, per field, WHICH compat flag(s) gate it and how each of those flags is independently resolved.

When porting one rung of a Pi detectCompat-style if/else-if DETECTION CHAIN (e.g. the thinkingFormat chain isDeepSeek > isZai > isTogether > isAntLing > isOpenRouter > "openai" in openai-completions.ts:1126-1136), match Pi's chain ORDER, not just the set of detected providers. pipy resolves thinking_format via its own ordered if-chain; appending a new check (together) AFTER an existing later-in-Pi check (openrouter) makes collision cases resolve differently from Pi (a row matching both a together provider/base-url and an openrouter.ai base-url should resolve to together, since Pi tests isTogether first). The different-family plan reviewer flagged exactly this and cost one plan-review round. In Phase 2 pin each ported detection rung's POSITION relative to its Pi siblings (including the deferred rungs like zai/ant-ling that fall through to the default), and insert the new branch at the Pi-faithful position; add a precedence test (together-provider + openrouter.ai base-url -> together shape). Skipped Pi rungs that pipy defers must not silently reorder the rungs pipy does implement.

Not every openai-completions thinkingFormat variant has a secondary reasoning_effort flag. The zai (and explicit-only qwen) branch is a BARE-BOOLEAN family: Pi emits enable_thinking = !!options.reasoningEffort and NOTHING else (openai-completions.ts:556-559) — it never reads compat.supportsReasoningEffort and never emits a top-level reasoning_effort, unlike the deepseek/together branches. So mechanically copying the deepseek/together slice (which gate reasoning_effort on the independently-resolved supportsReasoningEffort) over-ports zai: do NOT add a supportsReasoningEffort gate or a reasoning_effort emission to an enable_thinking-family branch. Note the trap: zai IS in detectCompat supportsReasoningEffort exclusion list, which tempts a zai-excluded -> omit-reasoning_effort framing, but the omission is STRUCTURAL to the branch (it never consults the flag), not a consequence of the exclusion. Pin per-variant in the plan whether a secondary flag even APPLIES, and for the enable_thinking family add the INVERSE test (explicit compat.supportsReasoningEffort=true on-state still omits reasoning_effort) instead of the deepseek-style explicit-format-on-excluded-provider mismatch test. Detection still follows the chain-order rule: zai auto-detects (isZai = provider zai / api.z.ai) and is ordered before together and openrouter; qwen/qwen-chat-template/ant-ling/string-thinking remain deferred.

An openai-completions thinkingFormat variant that is EXPLICIT-COMPAT-ONLY (no detectCompat rung) needs ZERO resolver work and NO precedence test — the inverse of the auto-detected-rung rule (lesson 0870dd). detectCompat thinkingFormat chain = isDeepSeek>isZai>isTogether>isAntLing>isOpenRouter>openai (openai-completions.ts:1126-1136); there is no isQwen/isStringThinking, so qwen, qwen-chat-template, and string-thinking are reachable ONLY via an explicit model.compat.thinkingFormat. pipy _resolve_thinking_format already returns any explicit compat.thinkingFormat verbatim in its first branch, so for these variants you add ONLY the request-shape elif branch(es) in provider_construction and change neither the resolver nor its docstring detection order, and you write NO detection-chain/precedence test (there is no collision row to disambiguate). Helper test specs must set compat={thinkingFormat: <variant>} explicitly. Contrast ant-ling, which IS in the chain (isAntLing) and therefore DOES need an ordered detection rung + precedence test when ported. In Phase 2 pin, per remaining variant, whether it is auto-detected (needs a rung at its Pi-faithful chain position) or explicit-only (request-shape branch only) so the diff is not over-built with an unused detection rung. qwen-chat-template also carries a Pi-forced literal preserve_thinking:true constant present in BOTH on/off sub-states, independent of the reasoning state — pin such constant companion fields alongside the toggled enable_thinking boolean.

Two ant-ling findings the different-family PLAN reviewer caught (two plan-review rounds). (1) Non-reasoning + thinkingLevelMap default-branch LEAK: the `elif thinking_format == X and bool(spec.reasoning):` gate that zai/together/qwen use can FALL THROUGH to the default `elif reasoning_value is not None: reasoning_effort = reasoning_value` for a NON-reasoning model that declares a thinkingLevelMap, because map_thinking_level keys off supported_thinking_levels (the MAP KEYS) and IGNORES model.reasoning, so reasoning_value is a non-None string for it -> pipy emits a top-level reasoning_effort where Pi (whose branch AND default both gate on model.reasoning) emits nothing. Fix: make the new branch consume ALL of its thinking_format cases (drop `and bool(spec.reasoning)` from the elif; put the reasoning check inside the value helper) and add a non-reasoning+map regression test asserting neither field. The existing zai/together/qwen branches still carry this latent divergence as a candidate follow-on. (2) ant-ling uses the RAW model.thinkingLevelMap?.[level] lookup with NO `?? options.reasoningEffort` fallback (openai-completions.ts:581-585), unlike deepseek/together/openrouter/string-thinking which all have the `?? level` fallback. So do NOT reuse pipy reasoning_value (= map_thinking_level), which falls back to the raw requested level when a model has no map -> would emit reasoning:{effort:<raw level>} where Pi emits nothing. Add a dedicated string-only raw-lookup helper and a no-thinkingLevelMap on-state test. Generalize: per branch, read the exact Pi value expression and check for the `?? level` fallback before reusing reasoning_value. ant-ling is also the only emitting completions variant with a fully SILENT off-state (its branch is gated on options.reasoningEffort, so off/unset emits neither reasoning nor reasoning_effort).
